MASTER_PAD_VERSION_INFO.MASTER_PAD_INFO: Portable Application Description, or PAD, is a data set that is used by shareware authors to disseminate information to anyone interested in their software products.

Program_Info.Program_System_Requirements: macOS 15.7 (Sequoia) or later. Apple Silicon or Intel processor.

Expire_Info.Expire_Other_Info: 7-day free trial with full functionality. No account required.

English.Keywords: image optimizer, batch image, image compression, image converter, jpg png webp heic avif, resize images, crop images, mac image optimizer, batch rename, image quality
English.Char_Desc_80: Batch optimize and convert JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, and AVIF images on your Mac
English.Char_Desc_250: Batch optimize and convert JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, and AVIF images with drag-and-drop simplicity. Resize, crop, rename, and compress with precise quality control. All processing happens locally on your Mac with zero cloud uploads.
English.Char_Desc_450: ImageCrush is a native macOS app for batch image optimization. Drag in files or entire folders, pick your output format and quality, and export. Features include concurrent processing with up to 8 threads, a built-in crop editor with 17 aspect ratio presets, custom export presets, batch renaming, before-and-after preview, and EXIF metadata control. All processing happens locally — images never leave your Mac.
English.Char_Desc_2000:
ImageCrush is a native macOS app for batch image optimization. Drag in files or entire folders, pick your output format and quality, and export — all without your images ever leaving your Mac.
Features include: - Batch process hundreds of images at once with up to 8 concurrent threads - Import JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, and AVIF; export to JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, or AVIF - Quality slider with precise control from 1-100% for lossy formats - Resize images with max width/height constraints while preserving aspect ratio - Built-in crop editor with 17 aspect ratio presets for social, web, print, and cinematic use - Scale factor export (1x, 2x, 3x) with automatic naming for Retina assets - Save export configurations as custom presets and set a default that loads on launch - Batch rename files with custom names, suffixes, and sequence numbering - Before and after preview with interactive side-by-side comparison slider - Pause, resume, and cancel batch operations at any time - EXIF metadata control — keep or strip with one click - Undo and redo across every operation - 100% offline processing — images never leave your Mac - Built with native Apple frameworks, optimized for Apple Silicon and Intel Macs - Apple Notarized and App Sandbox enabled - Automatic updates via Sparkle
